title: Conjecture 1.9 (Alon's Conjecture on Ramsey Cayley Graphs)
statement_text: There is a constant $C$ such that every finite group has a $C$-Ramsey Cayley graph.
context_text: Alon [2] conjectured that every finite group of order $N$ has a Cayley graph whose clique number and independence number are both $O(\log N)$. This was verified for cyclic groups by Green [23] using the uniform Cayley graph, but essentially nothing was known in the general case. Over groups such as $\mathbb{F}_{2}^{n}$, the uniform random Cayley graph cannot serve as a witness since its clique number is $\Omega(\log N \log\log N)$ with high probability.
paper_contribution: Proves Alon's conjecture for all abelian groups of order $N$ for almost all $N$; additionally, for finite vector spaces of order $N$ with characteristic $\equiv 1\pmod{4}$, proves the existence of a self-complementary Cayley graph with clique number and independence number both at most $(2+o(1))\log N$.
notes: The conjecture is also listed on the Open Problem Garden [ref 4 in the bibliography].
paper_title: On the clique number of random Cayley graphs and related topics

title: Conjecture 5.1
statement_text: There exists $C>0$ and a two-coloring of $\mathbb{F}_{2}^{n}\setminus\{0\}$ such that there is no subspace $H$ of size $Cn$ for which all nonzero elements of the subspace are monochromatic.
context_text: Alon's conjecture remains wide open for $\mathbb{F}_{2}^{n}$ and $\mathbb{F}_{3}^{n}$. The finite unions theorem rules out a two-coloring with no monochromatic subspace at all over $\mathbb{F}_{2}^{n}$ for large $n$, so progress requires understanding the quantitative threshold. Establishing this conjecture would be an important step toward proving Alon's conjecture for $\mathbb{F}_{2}^{n}$, though the true bound on the minimum monochromatic subspace size may be considerably smaller.
paper_title: On the clique number of random Cayley graphs and related topics
